name: Shoelace
description: A forward-thinking library of web components that works with all frameworks and CDNs

description.value: A forward-thinking library of web components that works with all frameworks and CDNs

initial-release.comment: Shoelace (web components version) first released in July 2020. Original Shoelace 1.0 was 2017 but was a different CSS-only library.

framework.comment: Framework-agnostic Web Components that work with any framework; official React wrappers provided; works with Vue, Angular, Svelte

styling-approach.comment: Uses Shadow DOM with CSS custom properties (--sl- prefix) for theming; CSS Parts for targeted customization

component-model.comment: Styled component library with professionally designed, ready-to-use components; not headless

typescript.comment: Built with TypeScript (94% TypeScript codebase); full type definitions provided

ssr-compatible.comment: SSR support via workarounds (dynamic imports with ssr: false in Next.js); Web Awesome v3 improving to ~98% SSR compatibility

rsc-compatible.comment: Limited RSC compatibility due to Web Components' SSR challenges; requires client-side wrappers for React

design-system.comment: Custom design system with design tokens; not based on Material, Ant, or other existing systems

dark-mode.comment: Built-in dark theme via sl-theme-dark class; supports auto-switching based on prefers-color-scheme

theming-system.comment: Comprehensive theming via CSS custom properties (design tokens) at :root level

css-variables.comment: Extensive use of CSS custom properties with --sl- prefix for global tokens and component-scoped properties

design-tokens.comment: Design tokens for typography (font families, sizes, weights, letter spacing, line height), colors, spacing, etc.

rtl-support.comment: RTL support via dir attribute on component; handled through @shoelace-style/localize library; includes rtlKeyframes for animations

component-count.comment: 60+ pre-built components including forms, feedback, navigation, overlays, and utilities

text-input.comment: sl-input component with variants for password, email, search, etc.

textarea.comment: sl-textarea component for multi-line text input

multi-select.comment: sl-select supports multiple selection via multiple attribute

combobox.comment: No dedicated combobox; select component is not searchable/filterable

checkbox.comment: sl-checkbox component with indeterminate state support

radio.comment: sl-radio and sl-radio-group components for radio button groups

switch-toggle.comment: sl-switch component for boolean toggle values

number-input.comment: Input component supports type='number' but lacks dedicated increment/decrement controls

tag-input.comment: No dedicated tag input component; sl-tag exists for display only

color-picker.comment: sl-color-picker component with full color selection UI

date-picker.comment: No date picker in current version; calendar component is proof of concept; unlikely before 2.0 stable

file-upload.comment: Not provided; use native file input

rating-input.comment: sl-rating component for star rating input

input-groups.comment: Not provided as distinct component; can use slots in custom implementations

split-button.comment: sl-split-button component for button with dropdown secondary actions

table.comment: Not provided; use native HTML table

tree-view.comment: sl-tree and sl-tree-item components with hierarchical structure, keyboard navigation, lazy loading support

list-view.comment: Not provided as styled list component

avatar.comment: sl-avatar component with image and fallback support

badge.comment: sl-badge component for small status indicators

tag-chip-pill.comment: sl-tag component for standalone labels with removable option

calendar.comment: Calendar component exists in proof-of-concept branch but not in stable release

alert.comment: sl-alert component for static alert/banner messages with variants

toast-notification.comment: No toast component; use alert or dialog for notifications

progress-circle.comment: sl-progress-ring component for circular progress indicator

spinner-loader.comment: sl-spinner component for indeterminate loading indicator

skeleton.comment: sl-skeleton component for content placeholder during loading

modal-dialog.comment: sl-dialog component with focus management and accessibility

drawer-sheet.comment: sl-drawer component for slide-in panel from edge

popover.comment: sl-popup component for floating content anchored to trigger

tooltip.comment: sl-tooltip component; known accessibility issues with WCAG 2.1 compliance

dropdown-menu.comment: sl-dropdown and sl-menu components for action menus; known screen reader issues

context-menu.comment: No dedicated right-click context menu component

tabs.comment: sl-tab-group, sl-tab, sl-tab-panel for horizontal tab-based content switching; known screen reader issues

vertical-tabs.comment: Tab group supports vertical placement via placement attribute

breadcrumb.comment: sl-breadcrumb and sl-breadcrumb-item for hierarchical navigation with RTL support

navigation-menu.comment: sl-menu with nested menu items supports navigation patterns

sidebar.comment: Not provided; use drawer or custom implementation

collapsible.comment: sl-details component for expandable/collapsible content sections

splitter.comment: sl-split-panel for resizable split panes with keyboard and mouse control

form-libraries.comment: No official form library bindings; components work with standard forms and Constraint Validation API

animation-libraries.comment: Built-in sl-animation component with ~100 presets based on Animate.css; uses Web Animations API

icon-libraries.comment: 1,500+ Bootstrap Icons built-in; supports Font Awesome, Lucide, Heroicons, Ionicons, Material Icons, Tabler via registerIconLibrary(); Shoelace acquired by Font Awesome

date-libraries.comment: No date components requiring external date libraries; includes sl-format-date utility using Intl.DateTimeFormat

aria-compliant.comment: Components built with WAI-ARIA patterns; handle ARIA attributes internally

keyboard-navigation.comment: Full keyboard navigation support; some components have known issues

screen-reader-tested.comment: Tested with NVDA, JAWS, and VoiceOver; several documented accessibility issues identified

focus-management.comment: Focus management including focus trapping; improved in newer versions using native <dialog>

wcag-level.comment: No specific WCAG level documented; acknowledges accessibility is ongoing effort rather than binary achievement

accessibility-rating.comment: Adequate - ARIA-compliant with keyboard navigation and screen reader testing, but has known accessibility issues with tooltips, menus, and tab panels; explicit commitment to continuous improvement

bundle-size-full.comment: Full bundle size varies significantly; single button adds ~232 KB; designed for tree-shaking via cherry-picking components

bundle-size-core.comment: Not applicable - individual components imported separately; no minimal core bundle

tree-shakeable.comment: Tree-shakeable when cherry-picking from component-specific files; importing from main entry causes larger bundles

zero-runtime.comment: JavaScript runtime required for Web Components functionality

lazy-loading.comment: CDN autoloader lazy loads components on demand; Tree component supports lazy loading with sl-lazy-load event

documentation-quality.comment: Excellent - comprehensive API docs with interactive examples, framework integration guides, customization guides, hand-curated demos; powered by Eleventy with JSDoc-generated technical docs

interactive-docs.comment: Documentation includes live interactive component examples throughout; no separate sandbox

storybook.comment: No Storybook; development occurs within local docs site for better integration and documentation

figma-kit.comment: Multiple community Figma UI kits available including official Shoelace UI Kit by creator Cory LaViska

cli-generator.comment: No CLI for users; internal scaffolding for component development; fundamentally a component library not generator like shadcn

active-maintenance.comment: Active maintenance; v2.20.1 released March 2025; transitioning to Web Awesome v3 (now in beta)

commercial-support.comment: Web Awesome Pro tier available ($149/year combined with Font Awesome Pro); free tier remains open source

style-override.comment: CSS Parts via ::part() selector for component internals; CSS custom properties for global and component-scoped styling; no className override due to Shadow DOM

compound-components.comment: Uses compound component pattern (e.g., sl-tab-group with sl-tab and sl-tab-panel)

render-props.comment: Web Components use slots rather than render props pattern

slots.comment: Extensive use of default and named slots for content injection

unstyled-mode.comment: Not unstyled - components ship with complete visual design; not a headless library
